texto: I shall never forget the peace of his hermitage amidst the eternal snows and the lesson he taught me: that we cannot face the Great Void before we have the strength and greatness to fill it with our entire being. Then the Void is not the negation merely of our limited personality, but the Plenum-Void which includes, embraces and nourishes it, like the womb of space in which the light moves eternally without ever being lost.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anagarika Govinda: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I shall never forget the peace of his hermitage amidst the eternal snows and the lesson he taught…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, buddhism, the-void pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de the-void.

frase_original: I shall never forget the peace of his hermitage amidst the eternal snows and the lesson he taught me: that we cannot face the Great Void before we have the strength and greatness to fill it with our entire being. Then the Void is not the negation merely of our limited personality, but the Plenum-Void which includes, embraces and nourishes it, like the womb of space in which the light moves eternally without ever being lost.

texto: The power of such a mountain is so great and yet so subtle that, without compulsion, people are drawn to it from near and far, as if by the force of some invisible magnet…this worshipful or religious attitude is not impressed by scientific facts, like figures of altitude, which are foremost in the mind of modern man. Nor is it motivated by the urge to ‘conquer’ the mountain.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anagarika Govinda: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a motivacao. O trecho “The power of such a mountain is so great and yet so subtle that, without compulsion, people are d…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, beauty-sacred-places, holiness pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de holiness e mountain.
